title: Insights from table-top exercises in Europe on AI safety and cyber misuse
summary: This report presents findings from table-top exercises with senior policymakers in Germany, the Netherlands, and France, identifying governance gaps and capability priorities for responding to AI-enabled cyberattack crises.

title: A federal law bans late voter roll purges. Republicans are pushing to reinterpret it
summary: There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.

title: In Colorado primaries, a democratic socialist wins; Bennet's governor bid ends
summary: Democrats nominated their candidate to try to unseat a Republican House member and tapped the state's attorney general as a frontrunner for governor.

title: How America has celebrated milestone birthdays, from world fairs to the World Cup
summary: Two founding fathers died on America's 50th birthday, which was marked with toasts and parades. Over time, anniversary celebrations became more extravagant — and more controversial.

title: Months after he sent a harsh email to ICE, agents tracked him to his home and a hotel
summary: Federal agents went to a Rochester man's home to warn him that an email he sent five months ago could be an illegal threat. He was on vacation but another agent found his hotel hundreds of miles away.

title: The majority of Americans are proud but worry about direction of the country
summary: As the U.S. approaches its 250th birthday, Americans reflect on the state of the nation in the latest NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.

title: Poll: Most Americans think the U.S. has strayed from its founding principles
summary: Most Americans believe the country has moved away from its founding principles as the nation prepares for its big 250th anniversary, a new PBS News/NPR/Marist poll finds. Eighty-three percent of U.S. adults feel America has strayed from the ideals the country was founded on two-and-a-half centuries ago. Among them, close to half of Americans — 47% — say the U.S. has "moved far away" from these principles, joined by...

title: U.S.-Cuba Relations
summary: 1959 – 2026 Since Fidel Castro’s ascent to power in 1959, U.S.-Cuba ties have endured a nuclear crisis, a long U.S. economic embargo, and persistent political hostilities. The diplomatic relationship thawed under President Barack Obama, but many restrictions have since been renewed.

title: Birthright Citizenship in the United States: What to Know
